source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A mass \( m = 50\, \mathrm{g} \) is dropped on a vertical spring of spring constant \( 500\, \mathrm{N/m} \) from a height \( h = 10\, \mathrm{cm} \) as shown in the figure. The mass sticks to the spring and executes simple harmonic oscillations after that. A concave mirror of focal length \( 12\, \mathrm{cm} \) facing the mass is fixed with its principle axis coinciding with the line of motion of the mass, its pole being at a distance of \( 30\, \mathrm{cm} \) from the free end of the spring. Find the length in which the image of the mass oscillates.

image_dependency: question explicitly says 'as shown in the figure'
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate intended visual reliance
the setup is spatial/optical (spring, mirror, axis alignment, pole distance), though much of the geometry is also described in text
multi_step: solution likely requires combining mechanics and optics: determine spring-mass oscillation extent, then map object-position variation to image-position variation
the question asks for the length in which the image oscillates, not a direct single-formula lookup
multiple given quantities (mass, spring constant, drop height, focal length, mirror distance) must be integrated
verifiability: answer is a specific numeric short_text value ('1.2 cm')
multiple-choice options are provided, enabling straightforward final-answer checking
the reasoning path is formula-based and can be checked stepwise from stated quantities; registry_passed=true

decision_reasons: question, image, and answer are all present, so the core semantics are well supported
asset registry passed with no recorded issues
initial scores are strong for multi-step reasoning and verifiability
image dependence is meaningful but not a blocker because the visual asset is available
